Key 2024 initiatives include: expanding our recruitment partnerships with HBCUs and Hispanic-Serving Institutions, targeting at least 4 new campus relationships by Q3 2024; implementing blind resume review for all Analyst and Consultant-level roles to reduce unconscious bias; and achieving a 40% representation goal for women and underrepresented minorities in Director-level and above roles by the end of 2025. Current representation data is published in Meridian's annual DEI Report, available on the company intranet.

All employees complete a 2-hour DEI Foundations training upon hire and a 1-hour annual refresher each October during DEI Awareness Month. Meridian also sponsors four employee resource groups (ERGs): the Women in Consulting Network, the BIPOC Professionals Alliance, the LGBTQ+ Allies Collective, and the Veterans and Military Families Group. Each ERG receives an annual programming budget of $2,500 and meets monthly. Employees interested in joining or leading an ERG should contact marcuswebb@meridianconsulting.com. Meridian conducts an annual pay equity audit using Syndio software, with results reviewed by the Executive Leadership Team and remediation actions taken where gaps of 2% or more are identified. We are a signatory to the CEO Action for Diversity & Inclusion pledge and publish our EEO-1 data publicly each year.

title: Employee Referral Bonus Program
content:
Meridian Consulting Group's Employee Referral Program rewards employees for helping us identify exceptional talent. Any full-time employee (excluding HR staff involved in the hiring process and members of the Executive Leadership Team) is eligible to earn a referral bonus when a candidate they refer is hired and completes 90 days of continuous employment. Referral bonuses are paid as follows: $1,500 for Analyst and Associate Consultant hires, $2,500 for Consultant and Senior Consultant hires, and $4,000 for Manager, Director, and Principal hires. Bonuses are paid via the next regular payroll cycle following the referred employee's 90th day and are subject to standard payroll taxes.

To submit a referral, employees must complete the Referral Submission Form in BambooHR before the candidate applies, ensuring the referral is formally logged prior to any application being received. Referrals submitted after a candidate has already applied will not be eligible for the bonus. Employees may refer a maximum of 10 candidates per calendar year. If a referred candidate is hired for a different role than originally referred for, the bonus amount corresponds to the role they are ultimately hired into. In the event that two employees refer the same candidate, the first-submitted referral in BambooHR will be recognized. Employees who leave Meridian before the 90-day mark of their referred hire are not eligible to receive the bonus. All referral questions should be directed to HR Coordinator Jamie Lin at jlin@meridianconsulting.com. The referral program is reviewed annually in January and may be adjusted based on hiring needs.

title: Parental Leave Policy Summary
content:
Meridian Consulting Group offers paid parental leave to support employees welcoming a new child through birth, adoption, or foster placement. All full-time employees who have completed at least 12 months of continuous service are eligible for parental leave benefits. Primary caregivers—regardless of gender—are entitled to 12 weeks of fully paid parental leave. Secondary caregivers are entitled to 4 weeks of fully paid parental leave. Leave may begin up to 2 weeks before the expected birth or placement date for the primary caregiver. Adoptive and foster parents receive the same benefits as biological parents; documentation from the adoption or foster agency must be provided to HR at least 4 weeks before the anticipated placement date when possible.

Employees are encouraged to notify HR Director Sandra Kowalski and their manager as early as practicable, and no later than 30 days before the anticipated leave start date. Parental leave runs concurrently with any applicable FMLA leave. After exhausting paid parental leave, employees may request an additional 4 weeks of unpaid leave, subject to manager and HR approval, to extend their time with their new child. Health benefits continue uninterrupted during all paid and approved unpaid parental leave. Upon return, employees will be reinstated to their same position or an equivalent role at the same compensation level. Employees returning from leave will have a re-onboarding check-in meeting with their manager during their first week back. Meridian also provides a lactation room (Room 114 in the Chicago office) and a flexible pumping schedule for nursing employees. Questions about parental leave should be directed to HR at hr@meridianconsulting.com.

title: Annual Performance Review Procedures
content:
Meridian Consulting Group conducts formal performance reviews on an annual cycle, with review periods running January 1 through December 31. The review process launches each year on January 10, with all self-evaluations due by January 24. Managers must complete written performance assessments for each direct report by February 7. HR Director Sandra Kowalski will facilitate calibration sessions with each practice area lead during the week of February 10–14 to ensure rating consistency across the firm. Performance is rated on a 5-point scale: 1 (Does Not Meet Expectations), 2 (Partially Meets Expectations), 3 (Meets Expectations), 4 (Exceeds Expectations), and 5 (Outstanding). A forced distribution guideline is applied firm-wide: no more than 15% of employees in any practice area may receive a rating of 5, and no more than 10% may receive a rating of 1 or 2 without documented performance improvement plans already in place.

Manager-employee review conversations must be completed and documented in BambooHR by March 1. Reviews must include: a summary of goal achievement from the prior year (goals are set during the prior year's review cycle), behavioral competency ratings across six dimensions (Client Impact, Collaboration, Communication, Initiative, Technical Excellence, and Leadership), and development goals for the coming year. All ratings of 1 or 2 must be accompanied by a written Performance Improvement Plan (PIP) drafted by the manager in collaboration with HR. Merit increase recommendations, which are tied to performance ratings, are submitted by managers to HR by March 7 and reviewed by the compensation committee before final approval by Managing Partner Elena Torres. Employees must sign their completed review in BambooHR to acknowledge receipt; a signature does not imply agreement. Employees who disagree with their rating may submit a written rebuttal to HR within 10 business days.

title: Employee Termination Procedures Guide
content:
This document outlines the internal procedures for managing voluntary and involuntary employee separations at Meridian Consulting Group. For voluntary resignations, employees are requested to provide a minimum of 2 weeks' written notice for individual contributor roles and 4 weeks for Manager-level and above. Upon receipt of a resignation, the manager must notify HR Director Sandra Kowalski within 24 hours. HR will initiate the offboarding workflow in BambooHR, which includes scheduling an exit interview, generating the separation letter, and notifying IT, Payroll, and Facilities to begin access revocation and equipment retrieval procedures.

For involuntary terminations, HR must be engaged before any termination conversation occurs. The manager must document the specific reason for termination and provide supporting evidence to HR at least 48 hours in advance. HR will review the case for legal compliance and consistency with past disciplinary actions. All involuntary terminations require sign-off from both HR Director Sandra Kowalski and the relevant Practice Area Lead. On the day of termination, the meeting should be conducted in person or via video call with both the manager and an HR representative present. The meeting should last no more than 15 minutes and follow the scripted talking points provided by HR. Following the meeting, IT will revoke all system access within 1 hour. The employee will receive a separation agreement and final paycheck information from HR within 24 hours of the termination meeting. Any severance terms (see Confidential Compensation Policy) will be communicated separately. All documentation related to the termination must be filed in the employee's secure HR record and retained for a minimum of 7 years per Meridian's record retention policy.

title: Training and Development Program Overview
content:
Meridian Consulting Group's Learning and Development (L&D) program is designed to build the skills and capabilities necessary for both individual career growth and firm-wide excellence. All employees have access to a curated curriculum in TalentLMS, which includes over 120 on-demand courses organized by role, competency level, and practice area. New hires must complete a mandatory 8-hour learning path within their first 30 days covering Meridian's consulting methodology (the Meridian 5-Phase Framework), project management fundamentals, data visualization best practices, and client communication standards. In addition, all employees are expected to complete a minimum of 20 hours of professional development annually, which is tracked in BambooHR and factored into annual performance reviews.

Meridian's L&D program includes four formal training cohorts per year: the Analyst Accelerator (Q1), the Consultant Excellence Series (Q2), the Leadership Foundations Program for Managers (Q3), and the Advanced Client Strategy Workshop for Senior Consultants and Directors (Q4). Each program runs 4–6 weeks and includes live virtual sessions, case-based learning, and a capstone project. External training reimbursement is available up to $2,000 per employee per year for pre-approved professional certifications, conferences, or continuing education courses relevant to the employee's role. Employees must submit an External Training Request Form in BambooHR at least 21 days before the training date and obtain manager and HR approval. Employees who receive reimbursement for certifications or degrees costing more than $1,000 must remain with Meridian for at least 12 months post-completion or repay a prorated portion of the reimbursement. The L&D calendar is maintained by HR Coordinator Jamie Lin and published on the company intranet each December.

title: Workplace Safety Procedures Manual
content:
Meridian Consulting Group is committed to maintaining a safe and healthy work environment for all employees, visitors, and contractors in accordance with OSHA standards and applicable local regulations. The designated Workplace Safety Officer is Operations Manager Carl Hendricks, who can be reached at chendricks@meridianconsulting.com or extension 204. All employees must complete the Workplace Safety Orientation module in TalentLMS within their first 5 days of employment and annually thereafter. Emergency evacuation routes are posted at all exits on each floor of the Chicago office (Suite 800, 222 West Adams Street). Fire drills are conducted twice per year—once in April and once in October—coordinated with building management.

In the event of a workplace injury or illness, the affected employee or a witness must report the incident to HR and Carl Hendricks within 4 hours of occurrence using the Incident Report Form, available on the intranet under Safety Resources. Incidents resulting in medical treatment beyond first aid must be recorded in OSHA Form 300 and reviewed by HR and Operations within 48 hours. First aid kits are located in the kitchen on the 8th floor, the copy room adjacent to the main conference center, and the reception desk. AED devices are located at reception and near the elevator bank. For incidents involving ergonomic concerns, employees may request a free workstation ergonomics assessment by contacting Carl Hendricks; assessments are typically completed within 5 business days. Remote employees should also complete the Remote Ergonomics Self-Assessment in TalentLMS annually. Any safety concerns, near-misses, or hazardous conditions should be reported immediately via the Safety Concern submission form on the intranet. Reports may be submitted anonymously.

title: Grievance and Complaint Procedures
content:
Meridian Consulting Group is committed to providing a respectful and fair workplace. Employees who believe they have experienced or witnessed harassment, discrimination, retaliation, or other policy violations have multiple avenues for reporting. Informal resolution is encouraged as a first step: the employee may speak directly with their manager, or if the complaint involves the manager, directly with HR Director Sandra Kowalski. If informal resolution is not appropriate or has been unsuccessful, the employee should submit a formal written complaint using the Grievance Submission Form available on the HR intranet portal. Complaints may also be submitted anonymously via the third-party Ethics Hotline (operated by EthicsPoint) at 1-800-555-0193 or at meridianconsulting.ethicspoint.com.

Upon receipt of a formal complaint, HR will acknowledge receipt within 2 business days and assign an investigator—either an internal HR representative or an external employment counsel, depending on the nature and severity of the complaint. The investigation will include interviews with the complainant, the respondent, and relevant witnesses, as well as a review of any documentary evidence. Investigations will be completed within 30 calendar days, with a written summary of findings provided to HR Director Sandra Kowalski and the relevant Practice Area Lead. The complainant will be notified of the outcome to the extent permitted by confidentiality obligations. Meridian strictly prohibits retaliation against any employee who submits a complaint in good faith. Any employee found to have engaged in retaliation will be subject to disciplinary action up to and including termination. All complaint files are stored securely in the employee relations folder on the HR SharePoint site with access limited to HR Director, CEO, and legal counsel.

title: Promotion Criteria and Process
content:
Promotions at Meridian Consulting Group are awarded based on demonstrated performance, readiness for increased responsibility, and business need. The standard promotion cycle runs annually alongside the performance review process, with promotion recommendations due to HR by March 7 following performance review calibration. Off-cycle promotions may be requested at any time but require written justification from the manager and approval from both the Practice Area Lead and HR Director Sandra Kowalski. Eligibility criteria for promotion include a minimum of 12 months in the current role, a performance rating of 4 or 5 in the most recent review cycle, demonstrated mastery of all core competencies at the current level, and no active disciplinary actions or PIPs.

Each career level (Analyst, Associate Consultant, Consultant, Senior Consultant, Manager, Senior Manager, Director, Principal) has a defined competency profile in Meridian's Career Framework document, available on the HR intranet. Managers should use the Career Framework to conduct structured promotion readiness conversations with employees at least twice per year—at mid-year check-ins and during annual reviews. When submitting a promotion recommendation, managers must complete the Promotion Recommendation Form in BambooHR, including specific examples of the employee's readiness behaviors mapped to the next-level competency profile, the proposed new title and compensation adjustment, and a client impact statement. HR will compile all promotion recommendations for review by the Compensation Committee, chaired by Managing Partner Elena Torres, during the last week of February. Approved promotions are effective April 1 each year for the standard cycle. Employees will be notified of their promotion outcome by March 15 to allow time for compensation processing.

title: Employee Assistance Program Details
content:
Meridian Consulting Group provides all employees and their household family members with access to the Employee Assistance Program (EAP), administered through LifeWorks by Morneau Shepell. The EAP is entirely confidential; Meridian does not receive any individually identifiable information about employee usage. Services are available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week by calling 1-800-387-4765 or visiting the LifeWorks portal at meridianconsulting.lifeworks.com (access code: MCG2024). The EAP provides up to 8 free counseling sessions per issue per year with licensed therapists for concerns including stress, anxiety, depression, relationship difficulties, grief, substance use, and work-related challenges. Sessions are available in-person, by phone, or via secure video.

Additional EAP services include: legal consultations (up to 30 minutes free per issue with a licensed attorney, plus 25% discounted fees thereafter); financial counseling sessions with a certified financial planner (up to 3 sessions per year); childcare and eldercare resource referrals; nutrition counseling; and a digital wellness library with thousands of on-demand resources including guided meditation, fitness programs, and resilience-building tools. HR communicates EAP availability to all employees during onboarding, in the annual benefits guide, and each January during Wellness Month. Managers who observe signs of distress in employees are encouraged to share EAP information in a supportive, non-coercive manner using the Manager Referral Guide available on the HR intranet. HR will send a firm-wide EAP reminder communication each October during Mental Health Awareness Month. The EAP contract with LifeWorks renews annually on June 1.

title: Exit Interview Procedures and Forms
content:
Exit interviews are conducted with all voluntarily separating employees as part of Meridian Consulting Group's ongoing commitment to understanding and improving employee experience and retention. HR Coordinator Jamie Lin is responsible for scheduling exit interviews within the first 3 business days of receiving a resignation notice. The interview should occur no later than the employee's penultimate day of employment and is conducted either in-person or via video call; it should last approximately 45 minutes. The exit interview uses a standardized question set covering: reasons for departure, overall satisfaction with role and compensation, quality of management and team dynamics, feedback on benefits and work environment, and suggestions for improvement. Question sets are reviewed annually by HR Director Sandra Kowalski and last revised on February 15, 2024.

In addition to the live interview, departing employees are emailed a link to an anonymous online exit survey via SurveyMonkey within 24 hours of their resignation. Both the interview notes and survey responses are compiled in an Exit Interview Summary Form and stored in the confidential HR SharePoint folder (accessible only to HR Director and Managing Partner). HR produces a quarterly Exit Interview Trend Report that aggregates themes, common departure reasons, and recommendations, which is shared with the Executive Leadership Team during the quarterly all-hands meeting. No individually identifying information is included in the trend report without the employee's express consent. Managers are not provided access to individual exit interview notes. Where exit interviews reveal systemic issues—such as patterns of management behavior, compensation gaps, or policy inconsistencies—HR Director Sandra Kowalski is responsible for initiating a formal review within 30 days of identifying the pattern.

title: Compensation Philosophy and Structure
content:
Meridian Consulting Group's compensation philosophy is anchored in the principle of paying competitively within the mid-market consulting segment while maintaining internal equity across roles, levels, and demographics. Meridian targets the 60th percentile of market compensation data as its baseline for base salary benchmarking, using the Radford Global Compensation Survey and the Management Consulting Association Compensation Report as primary data sources, both updated annually. Total compensation includes base salary, a performance-based annual bonus (target 10% of base for individual contributors, 15% for Managers, 20% for Directors and above), the 401(k) match, and benefits. Salary bands are established for each career level and are reviewed annually each December by the Compensation Committee, consisting of Managing Partner Elena Torres, HR Director Sandra Kowalski, and CFO Raymond Park.

Current approved salary bands (effective January 1, 2024) are as follows: Analyst ($62,000–$78,000), Associate Consultant ($78,000–$98,000), Consultant ($98,000–$125,000), Senior Consultant ($125,000–$155,000), Manager ($155,000–$190,000), Senior Manager ($185,000–$225,000), Director ($220,000–$275,000), and Principal ($270,000–$340,000). New hire offers should target the midpoint of the applicable band, with positioning above midpoint requiring written justification and CFO approval. Annual merit increases are budgeted at 3.5% of payroll for fiscal year 2024, with allocations tied to performance ratings: rating 5 (6–8%), rating 4 (3.5–5%), rating 3 (2–3%), rating 2 (0–1%), rating 1 (0%). The Compensation Committee reviews pay equity results from the annual Syndio audit; any gaps of 2% or greater between similarly situated employees will be addressed in the next payroll cycle. Salary band information is not shared with employees directly but managers are coached to discuss an employee's position within their band during career development conversations.

title: Severance and Reduction in Force Policy
content:
This document outlines Meridian Consulting Group's confidential policy governing severance pay and procedures in the event of involuntary terminations not related to cause, including reductions in force (RIF), position eliminations, and layoffs. Severance eligibility requires a minimum of 6 months of continuous employment at the time of separation and is contingent upon the employee's execution of a Separation Agreement and General Release within the applicable consideration period (21 days for individual separations, 45 days for group layoffs of 2 or more employees, per the OWBPA). Severance amounts are calculated as follows: 2 weeks of base pay for employees with 6–12 months of service, 4 weeks for 1–3 years, 8 weeks for 3–5 years, 12 weeks for 5–8 years, and 16 weeks for 8+ years. Director-level and above positions receive a minimum of 12 weeks regardless of tenure.

In a reduction in force scenario, the selection process must be documented using objective, role-based criteria (e.g., elimination of a business unit, specific skill set no longer required) to ensure legal defensibility. HR Director Sandra Kowalski and employment counsel must review the affected employee list for adverse impact against any protected class before any notifications are made. WARN Act compliance (60-day notice requirement for layoffs of 50+ employees) does not apply at Meridian's current size but will be monitored if the firm grows beyond 100 employees. All RIF decisions require approval from the Managing Partner and CFO. During a RIF, affected employees will receive a minimum of 5 business days' notice where operationally feasible. Outplacement services through Lee Hecht Harrison will be offered to all separated employees at no cost for 3 months (individual contributors) or 6 months (Manager and above). COBRA continuation notices will be issued within 14 days of the qualifying event. Severance payments are processed via regular payroll and are subject to applicable taxes. All severance files are retained for a minimum of 10 years.
